source: Chapter Approved Event Companion layouts (Veizla.gg cheat sheet)
setupSteps: Place objective markers per the deployment map (terrain footprints may count as objectives in 11th Edition).
Set up terrain pieces on the grey footprints — green/yellow walls show suggested ruin placement.
Agree Attacker and Defender battlefield edges; Attacker sets up first after Defender (Strike Force).
Use one of the three layout variants for this matchup (organiser's choice or dice roll).

variants.label: Variant 2
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Purge the Foe 2
variants.deployment: Diagonal quarters — central objective with quarter-circle cutouts

variants.label: Variant 3
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Purge the Foe 3
variants.deployment: Hammer and Anvil — 18" zones top/bottom, central skull objective

variants.label: Variant 1
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Priority Assets 1
variants.deployment: Diagonal corners — Attacker top-left, Defender bottom-right

variants.label: Variant 2
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Priority Assets 2
variants.deployment: Hammer and Anvil — 18" zones, five objectives

variants.label: Variant 3
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Priority Assets 3

variants.label: Variant 1
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Take and Hold 1
variants.deployment: Hammer and Anvil — 12" zones, staggered flanks

variants.label: Variant 2
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Take and Hold 2
variants.deployment: Hammer and Anvil — mirrored home objectives

variants.label: Variant 3
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Take and Hold 3
variants.deployment: Side deployment — 12" zones, three centre objectives

variants.label: Variant 2
variants.title: Take and Hold vs Disruption 2
variants.deployment: Hammer and Anvil — asymmetric depth zones
